blocks.content: The car ride was twelve minutes. Mr. Cole didn't say anything after the house. He doesn't fill silence — he just drives through it.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: I know what fine looks like. You're it.
He pulled up to the front. Lincoln High sat there like it had been waiting. Big, brick, not impressed with any of us.
Students moving in every direction. Some in groups, some already on phones, some just standing there like me trying to figure out the layout from the outside.
I heard Jonathan before I saw him.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: YO — that's Michaels. That's actually him.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: From the AAU film? The one with the four touchdowns?
I turned. Mr. Tanaka's car was just pulling up behind ours. Jonathan stepped out and the temperature outside changed. That's not an exaggeration — the energy on those steps shifted.
Mr. Cole was watching too. In the mirror. Quiet.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: He's going to be very famous. Or he's going to waste it. Nothing in between.
Jonathan was already talking to three people he'd never met. Laughing. Pointing at the building like he was giving a tour he'd never taken.
Mr. Cole pulled away the second I closed the door. He always does that.

blocks.prompt: Jonathan hasn't spotted you yet. Half the steps are watching him.

options.label: Wait. Watch how this works first.
Don't wait. Find your own way in.
